behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in backoff math/state progression for one policy object, not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed; deterministic inputs reproduce it. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not hide this, because no external dependency is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration/System are disqualified by the behavior text focusing only on the wait-time formula ("doubles... up to a configured maximum"), which is pure logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the backoff calculator/policy with consecutive failure counts and assert exact delay sequence and max-cap behavior.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: likely failure is at module boundary (HTTP response adapter -> retry classifier -> backoff policy), not only inside one function. REAL-INFRA: real network not required; stubbed responses can carry 429/status/header variants. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit of classifier may miss mapping mismatches between modules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is about identifying response type and computing wait time, which can be reproduced fully in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client + retry middleware + stub transport; return representative rate-limit responses and assert retry decision plus computed delay passed to scheduler.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is orchestration across timer/scheduler + request executor + response propagation. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with fake clock and in-process stub transport; no real HTTP needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only retry math can pass while resend wiring is broken. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior does not require real network conditions, only correct resend/orchestration semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that fails with rate-limit once then succeeds; assert same request payload is resent after delay and final success is returned to caller.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error may be lost at boundary between retry loop termination and public API error mapping. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required; repeated stubbed failures are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: unit test of error class alone would miss wrong mapping in integrated call path. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is about post-retry error typing, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run client with stub transport always returning rate-limit until max retries; assert thrown error is `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected typed fields.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when retry controller, quota tracker, and scheduler interact over multiple attempts. REAL-INFRA: fake timers + in-process stubs can detect non-termination deterministically; real OS/network not required. MOCK-HIDE: isolated unit tests may pass while integrated state machine loops forever. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI steps. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text scopes to internal retry/quota chain; no requirement for real subprocess/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Compose real retry/quota modules with fake clock and always-rate-limited stub transport; advance time and assert chain stops at configured bound and returns terminal error without unbounded scheduling.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is shared-state handoff across concurrent callers and quota manager modules. REAL-INFRA: behavior text explicitly says "same process"; no external OS/network primitive is required. MOCK-HIDE: unit test of bucket math alone can miss per-caller wiring creating accidental per-request buckets. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by "same process" and local async callers; this is in-process concurrency/state sharing.
behaviors.test_description: Create one client instance, fire concurrent `call()` invocations with stubbed transport, and assert aggregate quota decrements from one shared bucket rather than per-caller buckets.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering failures arise at queue + dispatcher + retry wake-up interaction, not just single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: in-process scheduler/fake timers can reproduce FIFO violations; no real OS/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit test of queue container alone can miss integration bug where dispatcher bypasses queue order. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text concerns submission/dispatch ordering semantics, reproducible without real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple blocked requests, release capacity over time, and assert send order matches original enqueue order under the integrated queue/dispatcher path.
